title: Threat Models, Untrusted Content, XSS, Paths, Templates, and CSP · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in explicit static-publishing trust and security policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers.
Disable or sanitize raw HTML according to content trust and parser configuration and test dangerous tags, attributes, URLs, and DOM identifiers.
Constrain content, include, template, output, and asset paths; reject traversal, symlink escape, special files, and output overlap.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Threat Model
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug publishing team handles case 7741 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers.

Inspect the Threat Model trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Threat Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers. For Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Threat Model, keep Threat Model connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Secret Privacy Scan · fault isolation · Debug Secret Privacy Scan
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug publishing team handles case 1265 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat every output byte as public, scan for credentials and private metadata, minimize analytics, and require consent and retention policy.

Which observation would disprove this suspected cause: “A private source repository keeps generated secrets private on public hosting.”?

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-defense-in-depth-zahlxw" that uses case 7925 to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Defense In Depth.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Defense In Depth into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Defense In Depth, keep Defense In Depth connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-defense-in-depth-zahlxw
# Competency: ssg-defense-in-depth.
# Case 7925: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_defense_in_depth_zahlxw(untrusted_cases: int = 12, rejected_or_encoded: int = 12, path_escapes: int = 0, secret_findings: int = 0, unsafe_urls: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_zahlxw = "zahlxw-wxlhaz-7925"
    assert evidence_variant_zahlxw
    evidence_axes_zahlxw = (
        "readerzahlxw",
        "source7925",
        "parsewxlhaz",
        "routezahlxw7925",
        "artifact7925zahlxw",
        "recoverywxlhaz7925",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_zahlxw)) == 6
    if untrusted_cases < 1 or rejected_or_encoded < 0:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "threat-cases"
    if rejected_or_encoded != untrusted_cases:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "injection-coverage"
    if path_escapes > 0 or secret_findings > 0:
        return False, path_escapes + secret_findings, "publication-leak"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    assert rejected_or_encoded == untrusted_cases
    return True, rejected_or_encoded, "bounded-trust-policy"
